title: fix: spread spore-to-seed upgrade across 42 slots
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Steady stream of seeds
gist.blurb: We've smoothed out the Spore-to-Seed upgrade process to run every four hours instead of all at once. No more traffic jams, just a steady, reliable stream of fresh seeds.
gist.summary: The automated Spore-to-Seed user upgrade process has been distributed across multiple daily time slots to prevent GitHub API rate limits and improve reliability.
gist.impact: New users will now see their accounts upgraded to the Seed tier more reliably and up to six times faster throughout the day.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee managing a smooth, steady flow of seeds on a small conveyor belt or watering a neat row of tiny seed sprouts, representing the new paced-out upgrade slots. The Monitor Robot checking a schedule.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny conductor's hat, carefully organizing a steady, smooth line of glowing green seeds on a small wooden conveyor belt. The chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby holding a pixelated clipboard with a schedule. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
